title: Historical Studies — eleven historical and operational case studies, including the manuscript Trilogy

fields.vision: A historical-studies index for manuscript, diplomacy, war theory, international law, peacebuilding, and operational case pages.
fields.mechanism: It distinguishes long-form scholarship, international-affairs historical analysis, and operational simulations instead of presenting all history-facing work as one kind of object.
fields.built_components: The eleven historical and operational study pages are represented here as a native index. Trilogy (the flagship) is the deepest at the full manuscript corpus across 72 chapters. The operational-simulation pages (Habitat, Pharm, Respite, Uscri) are complete case studies in their own right. The international-affairs pages (Cicc, Peace) carry substantial reference content. The long-form historical pages (Crusades, War Theory, Amfp) hold scholarly skeletons that continue to develop.
fields.source_utilization: Source use is organized by role: method authorities, domain evidence, technical background, implementation evidence, and claim limits. The page should show why each source matters; direct quotations and stronger factual claims still require exact passage anchors before publication.
fields.plan: Continue scholarly depth on the long-form historical surfaces. Bind every claim to its source register through the same source-register pipeline. The operational simulations are stable; the long-form historical work continues to develop case-study density and bibliographic binding.
fields.status: Status: The eleven historical and operational study pages are represented here as a native index. Trilogy (the flagship) is the deepest at the full manuscript corpus across 72 chapters. The operational-simulation pages (Habitat, Pharm, Respite, Uscri) are complete case studies in their own right. The international-affairs pages (Cicc, Peace) carry substantial reference content.
fields.remaining_gaps: Remaining gaps: continue scholarly depth on the long-form historical surfaces. Bind every claim to its source register through the same source-register pipeline.
fields.detailed_source_vision: An eleven-surface historical-studies pillar that pairs three traditions: long-form historical scholarship (the Trilogy, Crusades, War Theory, the Washington-Jefferson era of American foreign policy), international-affairs historical analysis (ICC complementarity jurisdiction, Track II diplomacy and confidence-building), and operational simulation cases (running a Habitat for Humanity build day, managing a high-volume pandemic vaccination site, running a substance-recovery respite facility, refugee-resettlement resource allocation). The pillar treats ?historical study? as a discipline that includes operational case work, not just archival reading.
fields.detailed_source_mechanism: The page works by linking visible components, source roles, and next-step gates. The eleven historical and operational study pages are represented here as a native index.

maintenance_rule: Update this blueprint before strengthening public claims on the website. Do not use word count, module count, or status percentage as a substitute for concrete project substance.
claim_ceiling_rule: Public claims may rise only when the built components, source use, status, and remaining gaps are updated here and reflected in the rendered page.

public_standard_check.Vision: This document supports the public account of Blueprint by preserving the project purpose, reader-facing claim, and professional-portfolio role.
public_standard_check.Mechanism: It connects source material, project components, implementation state, and next actions so the work can be inspected rather than merely asserted.
public_standard_check.What is built: The supporting evidence is the named page, blueprint, packet, project file, source record, or implementation page described in the document.
public_standard_check.Source use: Sources are used as method and source background, domain grounding, or project history; direct quotation and stronger factual claims require passage-level review.
public_standard_check.Plan: Bind claims to exact source anchors, visible implementation evidence, or a narrower public claim.
public_standard_check.Status: Current support record for the professional package unless explicitly marked archival.
public_standard_check.Remaining Gaps: Passage-level source check, implementation verification, citation cleanup, and proportional public wording remain open where evidence is incomplete.

public_pitch.what_it_is: A historical-studies index for manuscript, diplomacy, war theory, international law, peacebuilding, and operational case pages.
public_pitch.distinctive_mechanism: It distinguishes long-form scholarship, international-affairs historical analysis, and operational simulations instead of presenting all history-facing work as one kind of object.
public_pitch.why_it_matters: It makes the portfolio legible as applied historical reasoning: some pages are manuscripts, some are legal or diplomatic analyses, and some are scenario-based operational case studies.
public_pitch.pitch_integrity_note: Mechanism-first public pitch inserted after the Nexus Lab mechanism-erasure audit on 2026-05-13; source basis: rendered correction table.

pitch_integrity_audit.standard: object, distinctive mechanism, value, built state, and non-overclaim discipline must be visible.

researcher_process_map.final_use_case: It makes the portfolio legible as applied historical reasoning: some pages are manuscripts, some are legal or diplomatic analyses, and some are scenario-based operational case studies.
